articles.title: HUL Shares Jump As JP Morgan Remains Bullish On Stable Demand Outlook - Check Target Price
articles.description: Shares of Hindustan Unilever (HUL) rose on Wednesday, June 10 after JP Morgan maintained 'overweight' coverage on the stock citing stable demand outlook and organisational restructuring. Share price advanced 2.78% intraday to Rs 2,192 apiece.
articles.content:
Shares of Hindustan Unilever (HUL) rose on Wednesday, June 10 as JP Morgan maintained 'overweight' coverage on the stock citing stable demand outlook and organisational restructuring.
HUL share price advanced 2.78% intraday to Rs 2,192 apiece. The sc... [1216 chars]

articles.title: Iran Tensions; FMCG Stocks Lead – Outlook Business
articles.description: Indian stock markets opened higher on Wednesday as FMCG heavyweights like Hindustan Unilever and Reliance led gains, even as investors tracked fresh US-Iran tensions and rising crude prices. Sensex and Nifty advanced despite weak midcap and smallcap indices, metal pressure, and cautious global cues from Asian and US markets.
articles.content:
Indian benchmark indices opened higher on Wednesday, supported by gains in FMCG and heavyweight stocks, even as investors monitored fresh geopolitical tensions following new US strikes on Iranian targets.
At around 9:30 am, the BSE Sensex was up 380 ... [2683 chars]
articles.summary: Indian stock markets opened higher on Wednesday as FMCG heavyweights like Hindustan Unilever and Reliance led gains, even as investors tracked fresh US-Iran tensions and rising crude prices. At around 9:30 am, the BSE Sensex was up 380 ... [2683 chars]The positive start came despite renewed hostilities in West Asia after US forces reportedly carried out fresh strikes against Iranian targets in response to the downing of an American helicopter. However, the market drew comfort from relatively stable crude oil prices, which remained below recent highs.

articles.title: Horlicks Unveils New RTD Milkshake Crafted for Nourishment, Taste and Everyday Convenience
articles.description: Hindustan Unilever Limited launches Horlicks Milkshake, a ready-to-drink beverage targeted at Gen Z.
articles.content:
Priced at Rs. 20, the product is designed to offer Gen Z consumers a pocket-friendly beverage option that delivers both great taste and more mindful nutrition credentials.
As Gen Z consumers become increasingly conscious of what they consume, there i... [3098 chars]
articles.summary: As Gen Z consumers become increasingly conscious of what they consume, there is a growing shift toward beverages that balance enjoyment with more mindful choices. While indulgent drinks continue to remain part of everyday culture, consumers today are looking for options that fit more seamlessly into evolving lifestyles and habits. The campaign is being amplified through a robust 360-degree national rollout spanning television, digital, social media, influencer collaborations, large-scale sampling initiatives, outdoor advertising and high-impact consumer activations across key markets in India.

articles.title: Consumer goods majors eye stronger sales as inflation pushes both volumes and prices
articles.description: Consumer goods companies anticipate stronger sales growth. Inflation from the West Asia conflict is expected to boost both product volumes and prices. Companies like Hindustan Unilever and Dabur are raising prices to offset rising costs. This shift is projected to lead to better sales performance than initially forecast for the current fiscal year.

articles.title: Market Value of Top-10 Indian Firms Erodes by Rs 1.25 Lakh Cr in a Week
articles.description: Reliance Industries, Bharti Airtel, TCS, Bajaj Finance, Larsen & Toubro, LIC and Hindustan Unilever faced erosion from their valuation
articles.content: New Delhi: The combined market valuation of seven of the top-10 most-valued firms eroded by Rs 1.25 lakh crore last week, with Reliance Industries taking the biggest hit, in-line with a bearish trend in equities.Last week, the BSE benchmark Sensex de... [1796 chars]
articles.summary: Reliance Industries, Bharti Airtel, TCS, Bajaj Finance, Larsen & Toubro, LIC and Hindustan Unilever faced erosion from their valuationNew Delhi: The combined market valuation of seven of the top-10 most-valued firms eroded by Rs 1.25 lakh crore last week, with Reliance Industries taking the biggest hit, in-line with a bearish trend in equities.Last week, the BSE benchmark Sensex de... [1796 chars]New Delhi: The combined market valuation of seven of the top-10 most-valued firms eroded by Rs 1.25 lakh crore last week, with Reliance Industries taking the biggest hit, in-line with a bearish trend in equities.Last week, the BSE benchmark Sensex declined 532.4 points, or 0.71 per cent, and the NSE Nifty dipped 181.05 points, or 0.76 per cent. Concerns regarding the pace of monsoon advancement also weighed on investor confidence," Santosh Meena, Head of Research at Swastika Investmart Ltd, said. From the top-10 pack, Reliance Industries, Bharti Airtel, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Bajaj Finance, Larsen & Toubro, Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) and Hindustan Unilever faced erosion from their valuation, while HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and State Bank of India were the gainers.

articles.title: Stock Markets Hold on to Gains Post RBI Policy, Measures to Support Rupee
articles.description: Sensex and Nifty edge higher after RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25% and unveils measures to attract foreign capital, scrap taxes for eligible foreign investors and support the rupee. Track key gainers like Bajaj Finance, Hindustan Unilever, Adani Ports, Axis Bank and M&M, and what the policy means for markets, growth and inflation.
articles.content:
Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty quoted higher on Friday afternoon after the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) kept its benchmark repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, and announced a raft of measures to attract foreign capital and support the rupee.
The 30-shar... [2808 chars]
articles.summary: Sensex and Nifty edge higher after RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25% and unveils measures to attract foreign capital, scrap taxes for eligible foreign investors and support the rupee. Track key gainers like Bajaj Finance, Hindustan Unilever, Adani Ports, Axis Bank and M&M, and what the policy means for markets, growth and inflation.Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty quoted higher on Friday afternoon after the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) kept its benchmark repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, and announced a raft of measures to attract foreign capital and support the rupee. We expect 50bp of rate hike beginning in October.

articles.title: Closing Bell: Nifty at 23,360, Sensex down 117 pts on policy day; IT, metal drag, banks gain
articles.description: Among the Nifty constituents, Hindalco Industries, Wipro, Trent, Coal India, and TCS were the top losers. On the positive side, Adani Enterprises, Hindustan Unilever, Adani Ports, Bajaj Finance, and Axis Bank emerged as the top gainers. Sectorally, the Media index outperformed with a gain of 3.5%. PSU Banks, Realty, Consumer Durables, and Healthcare indices also advanced about 0.5% each. In contrast, Energy, IT, Metal, Oil & Gas, and Telecom indices declined between 0.5% and 1.5%. The broader market showed a mixed trend. The Nifty Midcap Index ended about 0.3% lower, while the Nifty Smallcap Index closed largely flat.
articles.content: Indian equity benchmarks ended marginally lower in a volatile trading session on June 5 as investors reacted to the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy decision. At close, the Sensex was down 116.67 points or 0.16 percent at 74,243.34, and the Ni... [25147 chars]
articles.summary: The ongoing range-bound price action suggests indecisiveness among market participants, with buyers emerging at lower levels while sellers continue to cap gains near higher zones. The banking benchmark index remained volatile and traded within an intraday range of nearly 740 points. Notably, Bank Nifty ended higher for the fourth consecutive session, gaining 0.35%, indicating buying interest emerging at lower levels.

articles.title: Hindustan Unilever sets June 30 for FY26 AGM via VC
articles.description: Hindustan Unilever Limited will hold its 93rd AGM on June 30, 2026, via video conferencing to approve the final dividend of ₹22 per share for FY26. The record date for e-voting and dividend eligibility is June 23, 2026, with remote e-voting open from June 25 to June 29.
articles.content: Hindustan Unilever Limited has scheduled its 93rd Annual General Meeting (AGM) for Tuesday, June 30, 2026, to be conducted via Video Conferencing (VC) and Other Audio Visual Means (OAVM). The meeting, which will approve the final dividend of ₹22 per ... [6418 chars]
articles.summary: Hindustan Unilever Limited will hold its 93rd AGM on June 30, 2026, via video conferencing to approve the final dividend of ₹22 per share for FY26. The company is scheduled to hold its 93rd Annual General Meeting (AGM) on Tuesday, June 30, 2026, at 2:00 PM IST via Video Conferencing (VC) and Other Audio Visual Means (OAVM). Additionally, a Senior Citizens Investor Cell has been established by the Registrar and Transfer Agent, KFin Technologies Limited, to assist shareholders above 60 years of age.

articles.title: HUL employees on permanent roll down 10.7% to 5,898 in FY26
articles.description: Hindustan Unilever Limited's permanent employee count will decrease by 10.7 percent to 5,898 by March 2026. The company is investing significantly in expanding manufacturing for premium products. Median employee remuneration is projected to rise by 6.08 percent. HUL is also focusing on workplace inclusivity, with women comprising 44 percent of its managerial staff.

articles.title: Hindustan Unilever Ltd narrows its bets as FMCG giant chases faster growth
articles.description: Hindustan Unilever Ltd focuses on premium growth, investing ₹2,000 crore in manufacturing to enhance its Beauty & Wellbeing and Home Care categories.
articles.content: Hindustan Unilever Ltd (HUL) is sharpening its focus on premium products, artificial intelligence-led operations, and targeted investments in high-growth categories as it seeks to accelerate volume-led growth amid an improving consumption environment... [3059 chars]
articles.summary: Hindustan Unilever Ltd focuses on premium growth, investing ₹2,000 crore in manufacturing to enhance its Beauty & Wellbeing and Home Care categories.Hindustan Unilever Ltd (HUL) is sharpening its focus on premium products, artificial intelligence-led operations, and targeted investments in high-growth categories as it seeks to accelerate volume-led growth amid an improving consumption environment... [3059 chars]Hindustan Unilever Ltd (HUL) is sharpening its focus on premium products, artificial intelligence-led operations, and targeted investments in high-growth categories as it seeks to accelerate volume-led growth amid an improving consumption environment, Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director Priya Nair said in her first annual letter to shareholders. HUL Chairman, Nitin Paranjpe pointed out that while “the near-term environment will continue to present challenges,” however, India had consistently demonstrated resilience, emerging stronger through periods of uncertainty. These include deeper consumer segmentation, building more desirable premium brands, strengthening its route-to-market capabilities and concentrating resources on a smaller number of high-growth opportunities.

articles.title: Hindustan Unilever Cuts Workforce by Nearly 9% in FY26 Amid Slow Demand
articles.description: According to the company's latest annual report, HUL's permanent workforce declined by 8.6 per cent to 7,499 employees as of March 31, 2026, from 8,202 employees a year ago.
articles.content:
Published By :
,
Last Updated:June 03, 2026, 17:20 IST
According to the company's latest annual report, HUL's permanent workforce declined by 8.6 per cent to 7,499 employees as of March 31, 2026, from 8,202 employees a year ago.
Rapid Read
FMCG giant... [3597 chars]
articles.summary: FMCG giant Hindustan Unilever Ltd (HUL) reduced both its permanent employee and worker strength during FY26 as India’s largest consumer goods company navigated a challenging consumption environment marked by sluggish demand growth. The workforce rationalisation comes at a time when consumer goods companies are increasingly focusing on productivity improvements and cost efficiencies to protect margins amid slower volume growth. The strong performance of these businesses highlights HUL’s increasing focus on premiumisation, wellness products and high-growth consumer segments as it seeks to accelerate growth amid a broader slowdown in household consumption.

articles.title: HUL Trims Permanent Staff by 8.6%, Worker Count Falls Too as Demand Slowdown Bites
articles.description: Hindustan Unilever (HUL) has cut its permanent staff by 8.6% and reduced worker count amid a demand slowdown in FY26, even as sales grew 5% and profits stayed flat. Learn how MD Priya Nair is driving volume-led growth, sharpening consumer segmentation, and reallocating resources to high-growth categories, while parent Unilever doubles down on India with new premium beauty, wellness and personal care brands.
articles.content:
FMCG major Hindustan Unilever (HUL) reduced both its permanent employee and worker count in fiscal year 2026 (FY26), as it navigated sluggish demand, with sales growth of 5% and flat profit after tax from continuing operations.

We now have a portfolio of super premium brands that will travel into India at the right time," Fernandez added.

articles.title: Domestic demand, reforms drive India's growth momentum despite near-term challenges: HUL
articles.description: NEW DELHI, June 3:  Hindustan Unilever Ltd (HUL) remains confident about India’s long-term growth prospects despite near-term economic challenges, with the FMCG major betting on rising consumption, premiumisation and digital transformation to drive future growth, Chairman Nitin Paranjpe said. articles.title: Hindustan Unilever files BRSR for FY26
articles.description: Hindustan Unilever Limited filed its Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report for FY26, reporting significant environmental improvements including a 99% reduction in CO2 emissions per tonne of production since 2008. The company operates on 100% renewable electricity and has achieved zero non-hazardous waste to landfill status across factories.
articles.content: Hindustan Unilever Limited filed its Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR) for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, with the stock exchanges. The report details the company's environmental performance, noting a 99% reduction in... [7225 chars]
articles.summary: Hindustan Unilever Limited filed its Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report for FY26, reporting significant environmental improvements including a 99% reduction in CO2 emissions per tonne of production since 2008. BSR & Co. LLP, Chartered Accountants, provided an Independent Practitioners’ Reasonable and Limited Assurance Report for the disclosed sustainability information. The Hindustan Unilever Foundation has enabled a cumulative water conservation potential of over 4.5 trillion litres since 2010.

articles.title: HUL sets up Unilever Fragrance Hub in Mumbai
articles.description: Hindustan Unilever Ltd. (HUL) has announced the inauguration of the new Unilever Fragrance Hub (UFH) in Mumbai reinforcing India’s role as a key growth market and R&D hub.
articles.content:
Hindustan Unilever Ltd. (HUL) has announced the inauguration of the new Unilever Fragrance Hub (UFH) in Mumbai reinforcing India’s role as a key growth market and R&D hub.
The India facility is the third global hub in Unilever’s network following the... [2004 chars]
articles.summary: Hindustan Unilever Ltd. (HUL) has announced the inauguration of the new Unilever Fragrance Hub (UFH) in Mumbai reinforcing India’s role as a key growth market and R&D hub.Hindustan Unilever Ltd. (HUL) has announced the inauguration of the new Unilever Fragrance Hub (UFH) in Mumbai reinforcing India’s role as a key growth market and R&D hub. The hub, set up at the IIT Bombay campus, combines consumer insight, advanced science and AI to accelerate innovation, enhance product experience, and support premiumisation across categories. “This enables teams to design, test and refine fragrances end-to-end, while collaborating seamlessly with other Unilever fragrance hubs globally,” he added.

articles.title: Mcap of 7 of top-10 valued firms erodes by Rs 1.54 lakh cr; Reliance takes biggest hit
articles.description: NEW DELHI, May 31:  The combined market valuation of seven of the top 10 valued firms eroded by Rs 1.54 lakh crore last week, with Reliance Industries taking the biggest hit. In a holiday-shortened last week, the BSE benchmark Sensex dropped 639.61 points, or 0.84 per cent, and the NSE Nifty declined 171.55 points, or 0.72 per cent. From the top 10 pack, Reliance Industries, HDFC Bank, Bharti Airtel, ICICI Bank, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Bajaj Finance and Hindustan Unilever […]

articles.description: Apple has grown rapidly in India over the last five years and has significantly closed the gap with Hindustan Unilever, and its competitor Samsung Electronics.
articles.content: Apple India is expected to make a record-breaking revenue for the fiscal year 2026 as Indians spend more on premium smartphones and less on traditional household goods. According to the Kotak Mahindra Mutual Fund report, Apple may also overtake Hindu... [2118 chars]

articles.title: Apple set to clock Rs 1.4 lakh crore revenue in FY26: Report
articles.description: India Business News: NEW DELHI: Apple is set to overtake FMCG major Hindustan Unilever in revenue, as affluent Indian consumers spend more on premium smartphones and digit.
articles.content: NEW DELHI: Apple is set to overtake FMCG major Hindustan Unilever in revenue, as affluent Indian consumers spend more on premium smartphones and digital lifestyles while growth in traditional household staples slows. A report by Kotak Mahindra Mutual... [2015 chars]
articles.summary: India Business News: NEW DELHI: Apple is set to overtake FMCG major Hindustan Unilever in revenue, as affluent Indian consumers spend more on premium smartphones and digit.NEW DELHI: Apple is set to overtake FMCG major Hindustan Unilever in revenue, as affluent Indian consumers spend more on premium smartphones and digital lifestyles while growth in traditional household staples slows. A report by Kotak Mahindra Mutual... [2015 chars]NEW DELHI: Apple is set to overtake FMCG major Hindustan Unilever in revenue, as affluent Indian consumers spend more on premium smartphones and digital lifestyles while growth in traditional household staples slows.A report by Kotak Mahindra Mutual Fund estimates Apple India's FY26 revenue at over Rs 1.4 lakh crore, more than twice HUL's projected Rs 64,468 crore. Apple did not respond to queries sent at the time of publication.While Apple's projected FY26 revenue would comfortably exceed HUL and several major consumer-facing companies, it still trails the likes of Reliance Industries, which reported annual revenue of over Rs 10 lakh crore, and Indian Oil Corporation, whose revenue crossed Rs 8 lakh crore.Among technology and electronics companies operating in India, Apple is expected to surpass Samsung Electronics, whose India revenue is estimated at over Rs 1.1 lakh crore across smartphones, consumer electronics and appliances.

articles.title: Sensex Rises 332.39 Points, Nifty up 23,747.40 On US-Iran Peace Talk Hopes
articles.description: On Friday, Sensex climbed 332.39 points to 75,507.09 in early trade; Nifty up 84.60 points to 23,747.40. On Thursday, Shares of Bajaj Finance, Infosys, and Tech Mahindra emerged among the top laggards on the Sensex. On the Nifty index, Bajaj Finance, Tech Mahindra, and Hindustan Unilever were among the major losers.
articles.content: Mumbai: On Friday, Sensex climbed 332.39 points to 75,507.09 in early trade; Nifty up 84.60 points to 23,747.40. Indian benchmark equity indices ended marginally lower on Thursday as losses in IT, FMCG, and financial stocks weighed on investor sentim... [1495 chars]

Mumbai: The Indian equity markets posted mild gains early on Friday tracking positive global cues, over optimism regarding US-Iran peace negotiations. Broader market activity shows an optimistic trend due to positive quarterly earnings from small and midcaps.
